title: Compose config merge with backup-before-write
action: Extend src/config/merge.rs with `pub fn merge_config_on_upgrade(path: &std::path::Path) -> Result<std::path::PathBuf, MergeUpgradeError>` composing the pieces already built: read the existing file at `path` as a string (a clear IO error variant if unreadable), compute the new-version default via self-update-merge-config-11's `default_config_json` (pass the same root-selection logic ensure_config already uses -- `super::home().join("projects")` if that directory exists, else `super::home()`), compute the merged JSON via self-update-merge-config-12's `merge_missing_fields`, then persist it via the EXISTING `super::write::backup_and_recreate(path, &merged)` -- do NOT write any new atomic-write or backup logic; reuse `backup_and_recreate` exactly as-is (D7: it already backs up before overwrite and validates through `Config::load_str` before persisting). NOTE (validating finding, record via a doc comment on merge_config_on_upgrade): `Config`'s deserialization uses `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]` -- so if the merged JSON ever contains a field the CURRENT Config schema doesn't recognize (only possible if a future version's Config struct removes a field an older one had -- has never happened yet, and full rename/removal handling is explicitly out of scope for v1 per D6), `backup_and_recreate`'s own `persist_validated` step will refuse to write it (the backup still lands, but the upgrade fails with a clear error) -- this is a SAFE fail-closed outcome, not silent corruption, but it means D6's 'orphan left untouched, no warning' promise holds at the merge_missing_fields layer only, not end-to-end through this compose function, for the one input shape that can't occur today. Name the three required unit tests exactly: `merge_config_on_upgrade_backs_up_before_writing`, `merge_config_on_upgrade_adds_missing_fields_and_keeps_existing_values`, `merge_config_on_upgrade_fails_closed_on_field_unknown_to_current_schema` (the third constructs a fixture existing-config string containing a field name `Config` doesn't recognize -- e.g. a value only a hypothetical future/removed field would have -- and asserts merge_config_on_upgrade returns an error rather than silently writing invalid-per-schema content) -- use `tempfile::tempdir()` for test fixtures, mirroring the existing test harness pattern in src/config/write.rs.

truths: Calling merge_config_on_upgrade on an existing config file creates a timestamped backup of the pre-merge content before any write, mirroring backup_and_recreate's existing guarantee
After merge_config_on_upgrade, a field the default adds is present with its default value, and a field the user already had keeps the user's original value

artifacts.substantive: adds merge_config_on_upgrade and MergeUpgradeError, composing default_config_json + merge_missing_fields + the existing backup_and_recreate, with the 2 named unit tests, no TODO stubs

key_links: merge_config_on_upgrade calls default_config_json (self-update-merge-config-11) and merge_missing_fields (self-update-merge-config-12) rather than reimplementing either
merge_config_on_upgrade calls the existing super::write::backup_and_recreate rather than any new write/backup logic
prohibitions: No new atomic-write or backup logic -- must reuse write.rs's existing backup_and_recreate/persist_validated unchanged
No changes to src/main.rs (CLI wiring is EP6's job)

verify: grep -q 'fn merge_config_on_upgrade' src/config/merge.rs && grep -q 'backup_and_recreate' src/config/merge.rs && grep -q 'fn merge_config_on_upgrade_backs_up_before_writing' src/config/merge.rs && grep -q 'fn merge_config_on_upgrade_adds_missing_fields_and_keeps_existing_values' src/config/merge.rs && grep -q 'fn merge_config_on_upgrade_fails_closed_on_field_unknown_to_current_schema' src/config/merge.rs && cargo test --quiet config

trace.outcome: Added merge_config_on_upgrade + MergeUpgradeError to src/config/merge.rs, composing default_config_json (cell-11) + merge_missing_fields (cell-12) + existing write::backup_and_recreate (D7), with the 3 named unit tests including the fail-closed deny_unknown_fields case.
